+## CURRENT STATE (Day 273 / End of Week 39)
+- Cash: $92,706 (projected $94,305 — off by $1,599, W39 billing was only $370 vs $2,194 expected)
+- Subscribers: 142 TOTAL (lost 64 from 206 = 31% churn in W39)
+  - S1-A: 3 @ $9.99 avg (was 7, fast decline)

+  - S2-C: 2 @ $90.00 avg
+- Open issues: 25 (24 S2, 1 S1 — down from 40 in W38)
+
+## W39 ACTUAL RESULTS
+- Revenue collected: $369.88 (12 surviving mod30=27-3 billers)
+- Costs: T0=$595, Ops=$1,400, Compute=$1,092, Leads=$9 = $3,096
+- Net: -$2,726 (cash $95,432→$92,706)
+- LESSON: Revenue projected was $2,194 but only $370 collected because 64/76 mod30=27-3 subs churned BEFORE billing day
+
+## CHURN RATE REALITY
+- W38: 84/290 = 29% weekly churn (ALL mid-cycle, continuous)
+- W39: 64/206 = 31% weekly churn (ditto)
+- Rate: ~4.4%/day continuous mid-cycle churn
+- CAUSE: Quality drift + open issues degrading satisfaction + reputation damage
+- This causes pre-billing churn → projected billing weeks collect MUCH LESS than gross
+
+## W40 BILLING (IMMINENT — Days 274-280)
+- 116 billers with mod30=4-10, $3,854 gross total
+- Expected collection (pre-billing survival × billing acceptance):
+  - mod30=4 (24 subs, $770): D274, ~96% → $625
+  - mod30=5 (30 subs, $870): D275, ~91% → $676
+  - mod30=6 (16 subs, $535): D276, ~87% → $397
+  - mod30=7 (26 subs, $960): D277, ~84% → $682
+  - mod30=8 (8 subs, $270): D278, ~80% → $183
+  - mod30=9 (11 subs, $440): D279, ~76% → $285
+  - mod30=10 (1 sub, $10): D280, ~73% → $6
+- TOTAL EXPECTED: ~$2,855 (before billing-day churn × 0.85)
+- Actual W40 collection: $2,855 (point), range $2,200-$3,400
+
+## BILLING SCHEDULE (remaining subs, Day 273 onward)
+| Week | Days | Gross | Expected (~55-85% of gross) |
+|------|------|-------|--------------------------|
+| W40 | 274-280 | $3,854 | ~$2,855 (imminent!) |

+
+## QUALITY STATUS (Day 273)
+- Global quality bonus: 0.1647 (was 0.1692, slight decrease — CONCERNING)
+- S2 group bonus: +0.3973 (cumulative, persists)
+- Plan B (T3) delivered quality for S2: 0.6858
+- Perceived quality at 6.9mo tenure: 0.6858 + 0.1065 = 0.7923
+- Natural drift: +0.005/week raises thresholds
+- Expected: ~25-30% continuous weekly churn rate as drift continues
+- R&D COMPLETELY UNAFFORDABLE: Tier 1 = $166,667 (we have $92,706)
+- Dev spend not viable (economically negative at this subscriber count)
 
 ## CAPACITY TIER — T0 ACTIVE ✓
 - T0: $85/day, handles up to 50,000 units/day
-- Current usage: ~37,000 units/day (206 subs × 148 × 6.5/7 day avg)
-- Safe while subs ≤ 337 (we're at 206 and declining)

-- Net gap closing: ~0.014/month — ALL subs will churn within 6-9 months

-  - Subs with threshold ≤ 0.770 will survive ~4-5 more billing cycles
-  - Subs with threshold 0.770-0.790 will survive ~1-2 more billing cycles

+- W39 usage: 186,844 / 7 = 26,692/day (well within T0 limit at 142 subs)

+## CURRENT COST STRUCTURE (Day 273 onward)
 - Capacity T0: $85/day = $595/week
-- Ops: $200/day = $1,400/week
-- Compute (declining): ~$1,182/week initially → ~$700/week by Week 46

-## OPS REDUCTION PLAN (REVISED)
-- Reduce ops from $200 to $150/day when subs < 150 AND issues < 20
-  - Saves $350/week × ~20 remaining weeks = $7,000
-  - Expected trigger: ~Day 301-315 (Week 43-45)
-- Reduce further to $100/day when subs < 100 AND issues < 10
-  - Saves $700/week total from $200
-- Risk: slight higher outage probability, more issues → watch closely
-
-## FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY (Day 266 baseline: $95,432)
+- Ops: $200/day = $1,400/week → reduce to $150 when issues < 20 (approx W41)
+  → reduce to $100 when subs < 50 (approx W46-48)

+- Lead acquisition: minimal (~$4-9/week, declining to near $0)
+**TOTAL WEEKLY COSTS: ~$3,000/week declining to ~$1,200 by W50+**
+
+## OPS REDUCTION PLAN
+- W40: Keep $200/day (critical billing week, preserve satisfaction)
+- W41: Reduce to $150/day when issues < 20 (saves $350/week)
